Class ElUtils

java.lang.Object
edu.iu.util.el.ElUtils
final class ElUtils extends Object
Internal parsing and JSON-selection operations shared by the expression evaluator.

  • Method Details

    • getIndexFrom

      static int getIndexFrom(String s, char c, int from)
      Finds the index of a character in a string, starting from a given position, while skipping over inline template blocks (<`...`}).

      Inline template blocks delimited by <` and `} (or a terminal `) are skipped; nesting is tracked so that only characters at depth 0 are considered when searching.

      Parameters:
      s - the string to search
      c - the character to search for, or ANY ('\0') to match the first occurrence of any control character
      from - the index to start searching from (inclusive)
      Returns:
      the index of the first matching character at depth 0, or -1 if not found

    • getCloseBracket

      static int getCloseBracket(String s, int from)
      Finds the closing bracket for a bracketed expression while accounting for nested bracket pairs.
      Parameters:
      s - expression to search
      from - index at which to begin searching, inclusive
      Returns:
      index of the matching closing bracket, or -1 when none is found

    • select

      static jakarta.json.JsonValue select(jakarta.json.JsonValue selected, jakarta.json.JsonValue pathElement)
      Selects a JSON value using a subexpression result as the path element.

      A null path element leaves selected unchanged. JSON strings and numbers are converted to text and delegated to select(JsonValue, String).

      Parameters:
      selected - value from which to select
      pathElement - string or numeric path element, or null
      Returns:
      selected JSON value
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if pathElement is not a string or number

    • select

      static jakarta.json.JsonValue select(jakarta.json.JsonValue selected, String pathElement)
      Selects a value from a JSON object or array.

      A path beginning with / is interpreted as a JSON Pointer. Otherwise, the path is used as an object property name or parsed as an array index. An empty path returns selected unchanged.

      Parameters:
      selected - object or array from which to select
      pathElement - JSON Pointer, property name, array index, or empty string
      Returns:
      selected JSON value
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if a non-empty path is applied to a scalar value or an array index is not numeric
